title: WebNami vs Medium — Own Your Blog, Keep Your Revenue
description: Medium takes a cut of your earnings and locks your audience behind a paywall. WebNami is free, self-hosted, and gives you full ownership of your content and readers.

hero.heading: Your blog, your audience, your revenue
hero.subheading: Medium puts your content behind a metered paywall, takes a cut of your earnings, and owns the relationship with your readers. WebNami is free, self-hosted, and gives you complete control — your content lives as plain files, and your audience is yours alone.

reasons.heading: You own your content
reasons.body: Every post you publish on Medium lives on Medium's servers under Medium's terms. WebNami stores your posts as plain Markdown files on your own machine — no platform can take them away.

reasons.heading: Your audience, not Medium's
reasons.body: Medium controls who sees your content and when. With WebNami your blog is your own domain — readers subscribe to you directly, not to a platform that can change the rules anytime.

reasons.heading: Better SEO ownership
reasons.body: Medium posts rank under medium.com, not your domain. WebNami gives every post its own canonical URL on your domain, building domain authority that belongs to you permanently.

reasons.heading: Keep 100% of your earnings
reasons.body: Medium's Partner Program takes a revenue cut and ties your income to their algorithm. WebNami is free — monetise however you choose with no platform in between.
